name: Nelson Mandela
role: ANC President; 1st President of South Africa (1994–1999)
quote: It always seems impossible until it's done. I have walked that long road to freedom. I have tried not to falter; I have made missteps along the way. But I have discovered the secret: after climbing a great hill, one only finds that there are many more hills to climb.

name: F.W. de Klerk
role: National Party leader; Last apartheid-era President (1989–1994)
quote: I apologise in my capacity as leader of the National Party to the millions who suffered wrenching disruption of forced removals... We were wrong. I apologise for the pain. (2020, revising earlier positions)

name: Cyril Ramaphosa
role: ANC President; President of South Africa (2018–present)
quote: The new dawn is upon us. We must work together to take our country forward. South Africa's best days are ahead — but only if we confront corruption, build the state's capacity, and invest in every South African.

name: Jacob Zuma
role: ANC President; President of South Africa (2009–2018); MKP founder
quote: I have never done anything to embarrass the ANC or the country. The charges against me are politically motivated by those who want to stop radical economic transformation. I will not be used as a tool to undermine the ANC.

name: Thabo Mbeki
role: ANC President; President of South Africa (1999–2008)
quote: The question that confronts us is: does AIDS kill? Of course it does... But at the same time we have to address the question: what are the conditions that create a fertile ground for HIV to spread? And those conditions are poverty.

name: Walter Sisulu
role: ANC Secretary-General; Rivonia trialist; liberation stalwart
quote: I have dedicated my life to the ANC and will do so until the day I die. There is no turning back. The ANC will rule South Africa. We will run this country. Apartheid will end.

name: Oliver Tambo
role: ANC President-in-exile (1967–1991); led liberation movement from Lusaka
quote: Our march to freedom is irreversible. We must not allow fear to stand in our way. The people of South Africa must be free. One settler, one bullet — but let us also remember: one child, one school, one future.

name: Steve Biko
role: Black Consciousness Movement founder; died in police custody 1977
quote: The most potent weapon in the hands of the oppressor is the mind of the oppressed. Black man, you are on your own. Black consciousness is an attitude of the mind and a way of life.

name: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela
role: ANC Women's League leader; 'Mother of the Nation'; Nelson Mandela's wife 1958–1996
quote: Together, hand in hand with our boxes of matches and our necklaces, we shall liberate this country. (1986, Munsieville speech — later condemned by ANC for endorsing necklacing). I am the product of the masses of my country and the product of my enemy.

name: Archbishop Desmond Tutu
role: Anglican Archbishop of Cape Town; TRC Chair; Nobel Peace Prize laureate (1984)
quote: My country is rich in the minerals and gems that lie beneath its soil, but I have always known that its greatest wealth is its people — richer than the deepest mine. Without forgiveness there is no future. Without memory there is no healing.

name: Chris Hani
role: SACP General Secretary; ANC/MK commander; assassinated April 10, 1993
quote: What I fear is that the liberators emerge as elitists who drive around in Mercedes Benzes and bring the crushing hand of apartheid on our people. What I fear is that the poverty of the many will lead to the revolt of the poor against the not-so-poor.

name: Hendrik Verwoerd
role: National Party PM (1958–1966); 'Architect of Apartheid'; assassinated 1966
quote: There is no place for [the Bantu] in the European community above the level of certain forms of labour. What is the use of teaching the Bantu child mathematics when it cannot use it in practice? That is quite absurd.

name: P.W. Botha
role: National Party PM (1978–1984) then State President (1984–1989); 'Die Groot Krokodil'
quote: The ANC is a communist organisation. I will not negotiate with murderers and terrorists. South Africa is under total onslaught — our response will be total strategy. I am not prepared to lead white South Africans and other minority groups on a road to abdication and suicide.

name: Julius Malema
role: EFF (Economic Freedom Fighters) Commander-in-Chief; former ANC Youth League President
quote: We are not calling for the slaughter of white people — at least for now. The expropriation of land without compensation is an act of justice and redress. We will nationalise the mines, the banks, and the land. The honeymoon of 1994 is over.

name: John Steenhuisen
role: Democratic Alliance (DA) Federal Leader (2020–present); Deputy President in GNU
quote: South Africa cannot afford another five years of ANC misrule. The GNU is our best chance to fix what is broken. We enter this coalition not as supplicants but as partners with a mandate for good governance, economic growth, and service delivery.

name: Chief Albert Luthuli
role: ANC President-General (1952–1967); Nobel Peace Prize laureate (1960); first African Nobel laureate
quote: The road to freedom is via the cross. I have been deprived of my chieftainship; simply because I persist in disobeying a government which is morally wrong and which has made racial discrimination its morality... I have a vision of a country where men and women, black and white, live and work together without fear.

name: Helen Zille
role: Democratic Alliance Federal Council Chair; Cape Town Mayor (2006–2009); DA leader (2007–2015)
quote: Our task is to make the Western Cape a beacon of what good governance looks like — to prove that South Africa can work when the right people and policies are in place. The ANC has failed the poor by serving itself. We will not repeat that mistake.

name: Thuli Madonsela
role: Public Protector of South Africa (2009–2016); author of 'Secure in Comfort' (Nkandla) and 'State of Capture'
quote: Accountability means that when those who have been entrusted with power abuse it, there are consequences. My job is to ensure that citizens' rights are protected — not the convenience of the powerful. State capture is a threat to democracy itself.

name: Joe Slovo
role: SACP Chairman; ANC/MK Chief of Staff; architect of sunset clauses in constitution
quote: The path from apartheid to democracy requires compromise that may seem painful now but will prove visionary. The sunset clauses — guaranteeing white civil servants their jobs for five years — were not surrender; they were the price of a peaceful transition. There is no revolution without power, and no power without realism.

name: Paul Mashatile
role: ANC Deputy President; Deputy President of South Africa (2023–present)
quote: The GNU reflects the maturity of South African democracy. We must build a capable, developmental state that works for all our people — not just the connected few. The ANC is reforming itself; the days of state capture are behind us.
